recipes.title: Ghormeh Sabzi
recipes.description: Iconic Persian herb stew of slow-cooked lamb, kidney beans, fenugreek, and dried limes served over saffron rice.
steps: Soak kidney beans overnight, then drain and simmer until tender.
Sauté chopped onion in oil until golden, then add cubed lamb and turmeric and brown thoroughly.
Finely chop parsley, cilantro, and chives, then fry in 3 tablespoons of oil for 15 minutes until very dark and fragrant.
Add the herbs to the lamb along with crumbled fenugreek leaves, dried limes pierced with a knife, salt, and pepper.
Cover with water and simmer gently for 1.5 hours.
Stir in the cooked beans and continue simmering for another 45 minutes until the stew is dark and thick.
Adjust salt and serve with steamed Persian rice.

recipes.title: Fesenjan
recipes.description: Rich Persian stew of chicken simmered in ground walnut and pomegranate molasses sauce until deep mahogany and tangy-sweet.
steps: Toast walnuts in a dry pan, then grind to a fine paste in a food processor.
Sauté finely chopped onions in oil until golden, then add the chicken and turmeric and brown.
Add the walnut paste and water, then simmer covered for 45 minutes, stirring often.
Stir in pomegranate molasses, cinnamon, sugar, and salt.
Continue simmering uncovered for another 45 minutes until the sauce darkens and the oil rises to the surface.
Adjust the balance with extra molasses or sugar to taste.
Serve hot with steamed Persian rice.

recipes.title: Tahdig
recipes.description: Crispy golden crust of Persian rice formed at the bottom of the pot, the most prized layer at any table.
steps: Rinse rice 5 times then soak with 2 tablespoons salt in plenty of water for at least 1 hour.
Bloom saffron in 3 tablespoons hot water and set aside.
Boil rice in salted water for 6 to 8 minutes until just al dente, then drain and rinse with cool water.
Mix one cup of par-boiled rice with yogurt, egg yolk, and 2 tablespoons of saffron water.
Heat oil and butter in a heavy non-stick pot, then spread the yogurt rice in an even layer for the tahdig.
Mound the remaining rice on top in a pyramid, then poke holes through with the handle of a spoon to release steam.
Wrap the lid in a clean tea towel and clamp on tight; cook on low heat for 50 minutes.
Drizzle the remaining saffron water over the surface, invert the pot onto a platter, and reveal the golden crust.

recipes.title: Chelo Kabab
recipes.description: Persian skewered ground lamb kababs grilled over charcoal, served with saffron-buttered rice and grilled tomato.
steps: Grate the onion and squeeze through a cloth, keeping only the pulp; mix with lamb, salt, pepper, and egg yolk.
Knead the meat for 10 minutes until very tacky, then refrigerate 1 hour.
Cook the basmati rice using the chelo method until fluffy with crispy tahdig at the base.
Bloom saffron in 2 tablespoons hot water.
Form the meat firmly around flat metal skewers, pressing it into long ridged sausages.
Grill over hot charcoal for 3 to 4 minutes per side until well charred but juicy.
Grill whole tomatoes alongside until blistered.
Serve the kababs over saffron-buttered rice with grilled tomatoes, butter, and sumac.

recipes.title: Ash Reshteh
recipes.description: Hearty Persian noodle soup of beans, lentils, herbs, and reshteh noodles topped with kashk, fried onions, and mint.
steps: Soak kidney beans and chickpeas overnight, then drain.
Simmer the soaked beans with 2 liters of water for 1 hour, then add lentils and cook 20 minutes.
Sauté half the chopped onion in oil with turmeric, then add to the pot.
Stir in chopped spinach, parsley, cilantro, and chives and simmer 30 minutes.
Add reshteh noodles and salt and cook 10 minutes until the noodles are soft and the soup is creamy.
Fry the remaining onion until deep brown for the topping.
Bloom dried mint in a few tablespoons of hot oil with sliced garlic.
Ladle soup into bowls, swirl with kashk, and top with fried onions, mint oil, and a drizzle more kashk.

recipes.title: Khoresh-e Bademjan
recipes.description: Persian eggplant and lamb stew with tomato, dried lime, and tart verjuice, simmered until silky and rich.
steps: Peel eggplants in stripes, salt them, and rest for 30 minutes; pat dry.
Fry the eggplants in oil on all sides until deep golden, then drain.
Sauté chopped onion in the same oil with turmeric, then add cubed lamb and brown well.
Stir in tomato paste and cook 2 minutes, then add chopped tomatoes and 500 ml water.
Drop in pierced dried limes, salt, and pepper, and simmer 1 hour.
Lay the fried eggplants on top, pour over verjuice, and simmer another 30 minutes uncovered.
Serve with steamed Persian rice and a spoonful of the dark sauce.

recipes.title: Joojeh Kabab
recipes.description: Saffron-marinated chicken skewers grilled until charred and juicy, brushed with saffron butter and lemon.
steps: Bloom saffron in 3 tablespoons hot water.
Grate the onion and squeeze through a cloth to extract its juice.
Whisk yogurt, onion juice, half the saffron water, lemon juice, oil, salt, and pepper.
Cube the chicken thighs and toss with the marinade; refrigerate at least 4 hours.
Thread chicken onto skewers and grill over hot charcoal for 12 minutes, turning often.
Brush the cooked kababs with melted butter mixed with the remaining saffron water.
Serve with grilled tomato, sumac, and saffron rice.

recipes.title: Mirza Ghasemi
recipes.description: Smoky Persian dip of fire-roasted eggplant mashed with garlic, tomato, turmeric, and eggs.
steps: Char eggplants directly over a flame until the skin is blackened and the flesh is collapsed.
Cool, peel, and mash the smoked eggplant flesh roughly.
Sauté thinly sliced garlic in oil with turmeric until very fragrant and just beginning to brown.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until reduced and jammy.
Stir the mashed eggplant into the pan and cook 10 minutes to dry it out.
Make a well in the center, crack in the eggs, and stir gently until just set.
Season with salt and pepper and serve hot with flatbread.

recipes.title: Sabzi Polo
recipes.description: Persian herbed rice with parsley, dill, cilantro, and chives, traditionally served with grilled fish at Nowruz.
steps: Rinse rice repeatedly then soak in salted water for 1 hour.
Finely chop parsley, dill, cilantro, and chives and toss with crumbled fenugreek and grated garlic.
Boil rice in salted water for 6 minutes until al dente, then drain and rinse.
Heat oil and butter in a non-stick pot and lay the first layer of rice on the bottom.
Layer the herbs and rice alternately, mounding into a dome.
Wrap the lid in a tea towel, clamp on, and cook on low heat for 50 minutes.
Bloom saffron in hot water and drizzle over the surface before serving.
Invert onto a platter to display the crisp tahdig and bright green herbed rice.

recipes.title: Halim
recipes.description: Slow-cooked Persian breakfast porridge of wheat berries and shredded lamb or turkey, topped with sugar, cinnamon, and butter.
steps: Soak wheat berries overnight in plenty of water.
Simmer the lamb shank with onion in 2 liters of water for 2 hours until very tender.
Remove the lamb, shred the meat off the bone, and discard the onion.
Add the soaked wheat to the broth and simmer for 2 hours until very soft.
Return the shredded lamb and continue cooking, beating with a wooden spoon, until the texture is smooth and stringy.
Season with salt and adjust thickness with hot water if needed.
Spoon into bowls, top with melted butter, sugar, and a generous dusting of cinnamon.

recipes.title: Tachin
recipes.description: Pressed saffron rice cake layered with chicken, yogurt, and barberries, baked into a golden crust.
steps: Simmer chicken with sliced onion, turmeric, and salt in just enough water for 25 minutes; reserve broth.
Boil washed rice in salted water for 6 minutes until al dente, then drain.
Bloom saffron in 3 tablespoons of hot broth.
Whisk yogurt, egg yolks, half the saffron water, and 30 g melted butter.
Fold the par-cooked rice into the yogurt mixture until evenly coated.
Press half the rice into a buttered baking dish, lay shredded chicken on top, then cover with the remaining rice.
Drizzle with remaining butter and the rest of the saffron water and cover tightly with foil.
Bake at 180C for 1 hour, then uncover and bake another 15 minutes; invert and top with barberries sautéed in butter.

recipes.title: Kashk-e Bademjan
recipes.description: Persian eggplant dip of smoky fried eggplant, garlic, mint oil, and tangy whey kashk, served warm with bread.
steps: Slice eggplants lengthwise, salt them, and rest 30 minutes; pat dry.
Fry the eggplants in oil until deep golden, then drain.
Sauté thinly sliced onions in the same oil with turmeric until caramelized.
Add minced garlic and chopped walnuts and cook 2 minutes more.
Mash the fried eggplants roughly into the pan and stir to combine.
Bloom dried mint briefly in a few tablespoons of hot oil for the garnish.
Spoon the eggplant onto a platter, swirl with kashk, and drizzle with mint oil and reserved fried onions.

recipes.title: Khoresh-e Gheymeh
recipes.description: Persian split pea and lamb stew flavored with dried lime and tomato, topped with crispy fried potatoes.
steps: Sauté chopped onion in oil with turmeric, then add cubed lamb and brown.
Stir in tomato paste, cook 2 minutes, then add 750 ml water and dried limes pierced with a knife.
Simmer covered for 1 hour until the lamb is tender.
Add the rinsed split peas and cook another 30 minutes until tender but holding shape.
Stir in cinnamon, salt, and pepper, then simmer uncovered to thicken.
Cut potatoes into thin batons and fry in hot oil until crisp and golden.
Serve the stew over Persian rice topped with the crispy potatoes.

recipes.title: Zereshk Polo
recipes.description: Saffron rice with tart barberries, slivered pistachios, and butter, traditionally served with tender chicken.
steps: Rinse rice and soak in salted water for 1 hour.
Boil the rice in plenty of salted water for 6 minutes until al dente, then drain.
Bloom saffron in 3 tablespoons hot water.
Heat 50 g butter in a non-stick pot and layer the rice into a mound; cook 50 minutes wrapped in a tea towel.
Rinse barberries and drain well.
Melt remaining butter with sugar in a small pan and toss the barberries for 2 minutes until shiny.
Drizzle saffron water over the cooked rice, then top with the buttered barberries and slivered pistachios and almonds.

recipes.title: Kuku Sabzi
recipes.description: Persian herb frittata packed with parsley, dill, cilantro, and walnut, baked until set and bright green.
steps: Finely chop all the herbs and spring onions.
Beat eggs with flour, turmeric, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Stir in herbs, chopped walnuts, and barberries.
Heat oil in a non-stick skillet, pour in the mixture, and smooth the top.
Cook covered on low heat for 20 minutes until the bottom is set and golden.
Slide onto a plate and flip back into the pan to cook the second side for 8 minutes.
Cut into wedges and serve warm or at room temperature with bread and yogurt.

recipes.title: Mast-o Khiar
recipes.description: Cool Persian yogurt and cucumber dip with mint, walnuts, and rose petals, served with rice or bread.
steps: Grate cucumbers, salt them, and let drain for 10 minutes; squeeze out excess water.
Whisk yogurt with grated garlic and a pinch of salt.
Stir in the drained cucumber, chopped walnuts, raisins, and dried mint.
Chill at least 30 minutes for the flavors to meld.
Sprinkle dried rose petals over the top and serve cold.
